task.title: Fixing unexpected routing behavior due to multiple default gateways

li.p: The host uses NetworkManager to manage network connections, which is the default.
The host has multiple network interfaces.
The host has multiple default gateways configured.

context.p: For most hosts, you require only a single default gateway to prevent unexpected routing behavior and asynchronous routing issues. Multiple default gateways are typically needed only for specific scenarios, such as when using Multipath TCP.

note.p: To route traffic to different internet providers, use policy-based routing instead of multiple default gateways.

li.codeblock: # ip -4 route default via 192.0.2.1 dev enp1s0 proto dhcp metric 101 default via 198.51.100.1 dev enp7s0 proto dhcp metric 102 ...
# ip -6 route default via 2001:db8:1::1 dev enp1s0 proto static ra 101 pref medium default via 2001:db8:2::1 dev enp7s0 proto static ra 102 pref medium ...

info.p: Entries starting with default indicate a default route. Note the interface names of these entries displayed next to dev.

step.cmd: Use the following commands to display the NetworkManager connections that use the interfaces you identified in the previous step:

info.codeblock:
# nmcli -f GENERAL.CONNECTION,IP4.GATEWAY,IP6.GATEWAY device show enp1s0 GENERAL.CONNECTION: Corporate-LAN IP4.GATEWAY: 192.0.2.1 IP6.GATEWAY: 2001:db8:1::1
# nmcli -f GENERAL.CONNECTION,IP4.GATEWAY,IP6.GATEWAY device show enp7s0 GENERAL.CONNECTION: Internet-Provider IP4.GATEWAY: 198.51.100.1 IP6.GATEWAY: 2001:db8:2::1
info.p: In these examples, the profiles named Corporate-LAN and Internet-Provider have the default gateways set. Because, in a local network, the default gateway is typically the host that is one hop closer to the internet, the rest of this procedure assumes that the default gateways in the Corporate-LAN are incorrect.

step.cmd: Configure that NetworkManager does not use the Corporate-LAN connection as the default route for IPv4 and IPv6 connections:

info.codeblock: # nmcli connection modify Corporate-LAN ipv4.never-default yes ipv6.never-default yes
info.p: Note that setting ipv4.never-default and ipv6.never-default to yes, automatically removes the default gateway’s IP address for the corresponding protocol from the connection profile.

li.p: Display the IPv4 and IPv6 routing tables and verify that only one default gateway is available for each protocol:

li.codeblock: # ip -4 route default via 198.51.100.1 dev enp7s0 proto dhcp metric 102 ...
# ip -6 route default via 2001:db8:2::1 dev enp7s0 proto ra metric 102 pref medium ...

p.xref: Configuring policy-based routing to define alternative routes
